info.title: bellatrixcloudtestrunner
info.description: Bellatrix is a cross-platform, easily customizable and extendable .NET test automation framework that increases tests’ reliability.

commands.name: runner
commands.description: Executes Meissa in --runner mode. You need to pass all native runner arguments using the argument nativeRunnerArguments.

options.name: --companyName
options.description: Needed to process your results correctly.

options.name: --library
options.description: The path to the tests' library file.

options.name: --licenseKey
options.description: Your BELLATRIX Cloud Runner license key.

options.name: --results
options.description: The path to the file where all results will be stored.

options.name: --testRunName
options.description: Needed to create the correct statistics for your runs.

options.name: --additionalData
options.description: An info that will be added to your test run notification such as URLs to CI runs and results.

options.name: --autoAnalysis
options.description: Mark whether to perform ML test failure analysis.

options.name: --filter
options.description: The filter that will be applied to the whole set of tests.

options.name: --maxCount
options.description: Specifies the maximum count of parallel test run processes. The default one is equal to the machines' CPU cores count.

options.name: --projectName
options.description: Needed to process your results correctly.

options.name: --reportPortalAccessToken
options.description: The Report Portal access token used for authentication.

options.name: --reportPortalLaunchName
options.description: The name of the launches imported to Report Portal.

options.name: --reportPortalProject
options.description: The name of the Report Portal project where the test results will be uploaded.

options.name: --reportPortalUrl
options.description: The Report Portal instance URL.

options.name: --retries
options.description: How many times the failed tests will be retried.

options.name: --runTimeout
options.description: The time in minutes after which the test run will be stopped. Default = 120 minutes.

options.name: --testRun
options.description: Specifies that this is a test run and no results should be published.

options.name: --testTimeout
options.description: The time in minutes after which the test will be stopped. Default = 5 minutes.

options.name: --threshold
options.description: If the percentage of the failed tests is bigger than the specified threshold %, no next reruns will be performed.

options.name: --timeBased
options.description: Specifies whether the tests will be distributed based on previous execution times.
